bristol_wpl_application: Every simulated WPL OBC file opens with simulation-only document control and no-real-world-reliance status
required_control: Validator requires Stage 32A simulation status phrase in front matter and simulated working draft
no_go_limit: Do not present as official Bristol WECA DfT or officer document

bristol_wpl_application: Use five-case structure with a report-purpose section explaining evidence maturity and blocked readiness
required_control: Each section must classify material claims as cited assumption inference proposal or gap
no_go_limit: Do not state that the WPL OBC is submitted approved or reliance-ready

bristol_wpl_application: Use regional and Bristol policy context to frame the problem and next-stage OBC purpose
required_control: Cite policy sources and keep WECA context separate from formal WPL approval
no_go_limit: Do not imply WECA/MCA support consent funding or no-role position

bristol_wpl_application: Present WPL options and package interfaces as draft/conditional and not selected
required_control: Boundary charge exemption and package choices must remain gaps until evidence closes
no_go_limit: Do not identify a preferred option boundary charge threshold or package

bristol_wpl_application: State appraisal approach and required data/model controls without invented BCR or VFM
required_control: Link to ASR OAR ASST model and uncertainty blockers
no_go_limit: Do not invent demand response revenue BCR VFM or monetised benefits

bristol_wpl_application: Separate Bristol CAZ-funded OBC development from future implementation funding and any WECA/MCA funding trigger
required_control: Package cost-line funding assurance classification required before reliance
no_go_limit: Do not claim Section 151 certification affordability WECA funding or capital approval

bristol_wpl_application: Describe delivery and procurement questions for licensing billing data enforcement customer contact and appeals as unresolved controls
required_control: Commercial readiness register required before OBC reliance
no_go_limit: Do not claim procurement route supplier readiness or service readiness

bristol_wpl_application: Use governance programme risk assurance and unitary/Combined Authority interface structure
required_control: Decision-route table must separate Bristol WECA DfT and Secretary of State roles
no_go_limit: Do not settle final order-maker submitter or WECA statutory role

bristol_wpl_application: Keep risks issues gaps and pitfalls live in the OBC body rather than hidden in registers
required_control: Stage 32A adds risk/pitfall rows for WECA-style overclaim and partial-exemplar overclaim
no_go_limit: Do not treat professional-looking draft as risk mitigation

bristol_wpl_application: Describe benefits plan monitoring and evaluation evidence required before reliance
required_control: Do not quantify benefits unless source/model backed; record baseline gaps
no_go_limit: Do not claim benefits are decision-grade or transferable from Nottingham

bristol_wpl_application: Use conditional language such as subject to future approval if approved and no-go until conditions close
required_control: Recommendations section must request only simulation conditions-to-close
no_go_limit: Do not recommend proceed approve submit launch consultation or implement

weca_example_evidence: SRC-BCC-0015 lines 879-886 and Stage 2H trigger map
bristol_wpl_application: Tag each WECA reference as strategic context funding/assurance trigger formal decision assumption or gap
required_control: WECA trigger tags required in exemplar register and OBC decision-route section
no_go_limit: Do not use WECA-style tone to imply approval support consent funding or no-role
